guardians.default_action: protect user intent and project scope; retrieve only materialized snippets through Simplicio
guardians.name: Isa
guardians.scope: user requests, project memory, docs, examples, active task context, and scoped retrieval

guardians.access_policy: no one bypasses Helo for runtime function knowledge, deterministic edits, gates, evidence, or handoff

decisions.reason: run `simplicio meta propose --repo . --json` for the full Helo learning pass over run evidence

guardians.default_action: own runtime execution/mutation; serialize writes, enforce gates, validate, and record evidence
guardians.name: Helo
guardians.scope: Simplicio commands, capabilities, schemas, workflows, evidence, gates, deterministic writers, and runtime decisions

guardians.access_policy: no direct neural SQL writes and no ungated internet/helper use; external knowledge is provenance-only until ingested

decisions.reason: Levi is armed but gated: external search/helper requests trigger only after Isa project memory and Helo runtime knowledge miss with an external/current-knowledge gap
Levi records append-only provenance and never writes neural SQL directly; findings re-enter through Simplicio memory ingest under Isa/Helo governance

guardians.default_action: prepare external acquisition only when Isa+Helo miss; record provenance and route writeback through memory ingest
guardians.name: Levi
guardians.scope: GitHub skills, governed sites/docs/articles/forums/news/social sources, and approved assistant-CLI consultation after a real gap

neural_guardians.handoff_policy: workers receive materialized context and commands through Simplicio; raw neural stores and mmap packs remain behind Isa/Helo
neural_guardians.index_policy: Isa/Helo index project memory, runtime functions, capabilities, schemas, workflows, runs, evidence, skills, and Levi provenance into local SQLite-first neural memory
instruction_flow: Isa receives and scopes the user request before project/user memory retrieval
Isa consults Helo whenever runtime commands, schemas, workflows, gates, or deterministic writers are needed
Helo owns runtime mutation and validation; provider LLMs may orient/review but do not bypass Simplicio execution
Levi is considered on every decision route but triggers only after Isa+Helo miss with an external/current-knowledge gap
Levi records provenance only; governed ingest brings approved findings back into SQL neural memory
Simplicio persists evidence, workflow status, leases, savings, and handoff before closure
neural_guardians.write_policy: Helo serializes runtime mutation through Simplicio deterministic writers and evidence gates; no provider session writes neural SQL directly

commands.name: doctor
commands.summary: check runtime, policy, adapters, model, repo

commands.name: map
commands.summary: map repository context before broad reasoning

commands.name: plan
commands.summary: produce an adaptive plan without executing

commands.name: decide
commands.summary: route through Isa/Helo memory first and avoid LLM calls when confidence is high

commands.name: guardians
commands.summary: show Isa/Helo/Levi guardian status, access policy, and full-stack default execution contract

commands.name: run
commands.summary: execute a task with validation and evidence

commands.name: sprint
commands.summary: schedule and run a sprint task graph

commands.name: resume
commands.summary: crash-safe resume of a previous run

commands.name: evidence
commands.summary: show run provenance and artifacts

commands.name: hbp
commands.summary: inspect the append-only evidence chain backing runtime proof and provenance

commands.name: validate
commands.summary: emit the progressive validation plan

commands.name: deliver
commands.summary: run delivery certificate, DOD, regression, and self-review gates before publish or closure

commands.name: gate

commands.summary: normalize a task into simplicio.task/v1

commands.name: taskflow

commands.summary: apply a mechanical edit plan and optionally run build/render/assert checks

commands.name: exec
commands.summary: route a gated Simplicio subcommand without opening a raw shell

commands.summary: check current CodeMap/file/symbol/test/patch compatibility before precedent replay

commands.name: savings
commands.summary: compare delivery with Simplicio vs a measured or estimated baseline

commands.name: computer-use
commands.summary: desktop capture/actions through explicit backend and approval policy

commands.name: status

backend_order.reason: lightest default for commands, files, Jira, logs, issues, names, and evidence
best offline neural-memory balance; keep SQLite and add semantic rerank
embedded/local option when the corpus grows or multimodal artifacts matter
robust offline vector search when a specialized retrieval layer is justified

write_policy: Isa is the gateway for project/user neural memory; Helo is the gateway for Simplicio runtime/function memory
orientation pack rebuilds are serialized generated writes
sqlite-vec semantic rerank when extension is installed
LanceDB/Qdrant only after corpus size justifies heavier backend
memory_backend.recommended_next: use SQLite FTS5 now; install sqlite-vec only when semantic recall is needed

limits.reason: profile_active_cap,cpu_thread_capacity,cpu_headroom_reserved,cpu_pressure: 32/600 logical agents active, 32 model worker(s), action=resume normal dispatch

editing.llm_role: orient, decompose, review, escalate, and produce structured plans
editing.editor: simplicio edit as the mechanical writer; simplicio-dev-cli as the focused dev/test loop that may call edit; simplicio run as the orchestrator
editing.note: provider sessions must not bypass Simplicio for code mutation

escalation.source: existing simplicio-prompt fan-out policy organized in simplicio-runtime

local_attempts.success_gate: focused validation, evidence, and taskflow pass; full suite is pre-commit only
local_attempts.failure_gate: focused validation failure, blocker, consensus failure, or incomplete evidence

local_attempts.success_gate: focused validation, evidence, and taskflow pass; full suite is pre-commit only
local_attempts.failure_gate: focused validation failure, blocker, consensus failure, or incomplete evidence

local_attempts.success_gate: focused validation, evidence, and taskflow pass; full suite is pre-commit only
local_attempts.failure_gate: focused validation failure, blocker, consensus failure, or incomplete evidence

local_attempts.success_gate: focused validation, evidence, and taskflow pass; full suite is pre-commit only
local_attempts.failure_gate: focused validation failure, blocker, consensus failure, or incomplete evidence

escalation.memory_guard: logical subagents share the governed local model pool; do not spawn raw llama.cpp workers outside Simplicio

paid_fallback.allowed_after: all local attempts 64, 100, 200, and 600 fail or remain blocked
paid_fallback.requires: explicit --remote or --allow-remote policy, configured paid provider, and recorded escalation evidence
paid_fallback.audit: record escalation reason, provider, cost, tests, and evidence in the source task and Simplicio board

llama_cpp_policy.reason: prevent llama.cpp memory spikes while allowing many logical agents to share a governed 256-worker local model pool

default_llm_policy.applies_when: the task is mechanical, repetitive, stable programming work, or answerable from local memory/model knowledge
default_llm_policy.enable_thinking_when: the task explicitly asks for deeper reasoning, is ambiguous, high-risk, architectural, security/release sensitive, or deterministic/local attempts fail
default_llm_policy.enable_internet_when: the task explicitly needs current/external knowledge such as package downloads, GitHub/CI verification, current docs, security advisories, prices/news, or Levi-approved external acquisition after Isa/Helo miss
default_llm_policy.tools_default: minimum necessary Simplicio tools for local inspection, execution, validation, evidence, and deterministic edits
default_llm_policy.skills_default: skills are allowed when useful; rank/recall first and lazy-load only relevant selected skills
default_llm_policy.speed_goal: prefer the most direct deterministic/local path with the fewest external dependencies

token_cost_guidance: default to short/no-think/offline with minimum necessary tools for mechanical or memory-known work; enable deeper reasoning and internet only when the task explicitly requires external/current knowledge, higher-risk judgment, or local deterministic attempts fail
examples: map before broad repo reasoning: simplicio map --repo . --json
task graph/status with sprint: simplicio sprint sprint.md --repo .
standard IO smoke chain: simplicio contracts smoke --json
local memory backend plan: simplicio memory status --json
zero-copy orientation pack: simplicio orientation pack --repo . --json
decided mechanical edit, zero tokens: simplicio edit --plan plan.json
background compiled update policy: simplicio update auto status --json

ownership.do_not_duplicate: reuse first-party adapters and built-in tools before adding new skills
